Following the victory, Vijay penned an open letter to the public, criticizing the opposition DMK for attempting to undermine his administration. He highlighted his party's electoral performance and commitment to secular values, while accusing the DMK of hypocrisy in coalition politics.

title: Sanjay Gupta on Rs 300 crore ‘Dhurandhar’ gamble with Ranveer Singh
description: Filmmaker Sanjay Gupta praised Aditya Dhar's vision behind 'Dhurandhar', calling it a massive risk given Ranveer Singh's career low and Dhar's limited filmmaking experience. He credited the Ambanis' backing as a key factor in the film's success. The two-part franchise has reportedly crossed Rs 3000 crore worldwide.

title: Trump in China to meet President Xi to talk trade, Iran war
Patience as power: How Beijing turned America's Iran war into a Taiwan strategy
description: While the US and Israel engaged in conflict with Iran, China quietly leveraged the situation to expand its influence. By positioning itself as a diplomatic alternative and stabilizing energy supplies, Beijing benefited from a weakened American strategic position and growing uncertainty about US reliability among allies.

For KKR, it's a damaging blow, pushing them to seventh and demanding wins in all remaining games after an earlier tied contest.

Alexander Zverev denied entry at Rome restaurant before Italian Open loss

Reports also claimed that the restaurant staff failed to recognise the German tennis star when he arrived with his brother Mischa Zverev and a friend for dinner.

The airline will continue operating over 1,200 international flights monthly across five continents.

Empty offices, locked buildings & fake work location: US agency flags OPT visa fraud
description: US immigration agencies have uncovered widespread fraud within the Optional Practical Training (OPT) program, involving thousands of foreign students and suspect employers. Investigations revealed fake offices, empty buildings, and companies claiming to employ hundreds with little to no actual business activity, suggesting a "guestworker pipeline" issue.

title: Muhammad Ali’s powerful quote on life, struggle and resilience
description: Muhammad Ali’s most powerful words did not come during his boxing prime, but years after Parkinson’s disease changed his body and voice. The article explores how Ali faced physical decline with dignity, resilience and purpose, refusing to let illness define his value. Through personal struggles, public appearances and charity work, Ali showed that strength is not only about dominance, but continuing anyway.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sold their electrical co for $1.7bn and gave 540 workers $240 m of it
description: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.
